notes.sourceDetail: chapter XXIV, Zaynab of Zanján, pp. 775-784
notes.recentPressure: The Dawn-Breakers is now warm again after recent chapter III and chapter XXIV use, and early Bábí martyrdom/courage material needs a rest. This run intentionally accepted the repetition cost because Zaynab answered the underused women/equality target with unusually high narrative force.

notes.searchCorrection: Zaynab beat Bowery Mission/Promulgation and Mona-related periodical material on completeness, source prestige, image fit, and memorability. Tomorrow should not lean on another siege, martyrdom, or Dawn-Breakers rescue unless it is extraordinary and clearly different.

notes.sourceDetail: Vol. 15, Issue 11, Martha Root on Hyde and Clara Dunn in Australia and New Zealand
notes.recentPressure: Star of the West now has two recent strong uses, and today's selection is another early Western pioneer/teaching report. It successfully avoided Bahá’í World and wartime Haifa, but it should not become the next default fallback.

notes.searchCorrection: Dunn won because it was a complete founding-community story with a direct archival image. Agnes Alexander's Tokyo earthquake, Keith Ransom-Kehler material, and Promulgation race-unity talks were useful comparisons but lost for disaster-service adjacency, biography/eulogy shape, or discourse-heavy structure.

notes.recentPressure: The Chosen Highway refreshed the source mix after A Traveler’s Narrative and The Priceless Pearl, but it still centers ‘Abdu’l-Bahá, wartime Palestine, and heroic message-bearing. Steadfastness has now appeared recently, so avoid another danger-and-letter-carrying arc next.

notes.searchCorrection: The Star of the West Garden of Healing and Chicago healing reminiscence were useful comparisons but weaker: one leaned service-heavy and diffuse, the other repeated ‘Abdu’l-Bahá healing/pilgrimage patterns. Haji Ramadan won because the episode is complete, vivid, and imageable.

notes.sourceDetail: part 2, Siyyid Yaḥyá of Daráb and Mullá Muḥammad-‘Alí of Zanján recognizing the Báb’s claim
notes.recentPressure: A Traveler’s Narrative corrected the recent Bahá’í World/service pressure and avoided another ‘Abdu’l-Bahá/Shoghi Effendi reminiscence. The theme is transformation, but early Bábí recognition and Báb-centered material should now rest.

notes.searchCorrection: The Dawn-Breakers Mákú scene was the closest runner-up and has direct image fit, but recent Dawn-Breakers/Báb usage made A Traveler’s Narrative the better rotation today.
